{{Quote|We're still pinned down by that superlaser!|[[General/Legends|General]] [[Crix Madine/Legends|Crix Madine]]|<ref name="Rebel Strike" />}}
The '''Battle of Dubrillion''' was a battle during the mid-[[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]. The battle, in the midst of the [[Dubrillion Ocean]], was an attempt by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] to draw in and destroy a Rebel Alliance task force commanded by [[General/Legends|General]] [[Crix Madine/Legends|Crix Madine]] and deal a crippling blow to the [[Alliance Special Forces/Legends|Alliance Special Forces]].

==Prelude==
The [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance's]] original [[Mission to Destrillion|operation]] in the [[Dubrillion system/Legends|Dubrillion system]] had seemingly ended in failure. [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] and his [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wing]] squadrons had searched out the energy tunnels in the rings of [[Destrillion]] for the location of a [[Tibanna/Legends|tibanna]] gas mining facility which had discovered by [[Alliance Intelligence/Legends|Alliance Intelligence]]. When no facility was found and the Alliance fleet led by a [[MC80 Liberty Type Heavy Star Cruiser/Legends|MC80 Star Cruiser]] was about to leave the system, they regrouped and passed through [[Dubrillion/Legends|Dubrillion's]] orbit in order to enter [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]. In truth, however, the Empire had planned the Rebels' escape from the Dubrillion system, and when the Alliance fleet was caught in orbit<ref name="Rebel Strike" /> and unable to exfiltrate, the Imperials sprung their trap.

==The battle==
===Bombing runs===
{{Quote|Come on, Rogue Group. You didn't think we were finished, did you?|Antilles to the Rogues|<ref name="Rebel Strike" />}}
[[File:Guns_of_dubrillion.jpg|thumb|left|150px|The superlaser's main component is destroyed.]]
The Alliance convoy was fired upon by an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Superlaser/Legends|superlaser]] on [[Dubrillion/Legends|Dubrillion's]] surface, which destroyed a [[GR-75 medium transport/Legends|GR-75 medium transport]]. As the fleet was not able to do a safe jump into hyperspace, [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] was ordered to lead a [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wing]] [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] down to the planet. Wedge, when telling Rogue Squadron to descend down onto the planet, also referenced [[Mission to Destrillion|their earlier mission]] by telling them they weren't finished yet. By the time the squadron reached the superlaser, Madine informed them about three targeting relays the weapon used.<ref name="Rebel Strike" />

Alliance squadrons swarmed into the [[Dubrillion superlaser facility]], and came under attack by [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]] and [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE/IN interceptors]]. Along with the fighters, [[Imperial submarine/Legends|Imperial submarines]] emerged from the [[Dubrillion Ocean]]. Antilles led a bombing run on the three relay dishes for the superlaser, to reduce the accuracy of the main laser. He disabled the shields on the three relays with his [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannon]], then dropped [[Proton bomb/Legends|proton bombs]] on each, destroying them.<ref name="Rebel Strike" />

{{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike}}
During the mission, Wedge Antilles spotted an object within the main superlaser dish. He sent his Y-wing down and managed to recover some [[advanced spread proton bomb]]s.<ref name="Rebel Strike" />
{{Endgame}}

While the accuracy of the superweapon was drastically reduced, the Alliance fleet was still taking casualties. In spite of the move to a safer orbit, the fleet was still pinned down.<ref name="Rebel Strike" /> As the facility was also designed to withstand aerial assault,<ref name="RS III guide">''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike: The Official Nintendo Player's Guide]]''</ref> Antilles was ordered to go deep into the facility and shut it down, while his wingmen covered him from the air.<ref name="Rebel Strike" />

===Infiltration===
In the midst of the battle, Antilles apprehended an [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|All Terrain Scout Transport]] by causing an ion cannon blast to skim across its circuitry, not disabling the vehicle but causing it to temporarily malfunction. Upon hijacking it, General Madine reported that they had scientists who were familiar with the facility's layout, and patched one of them through. The scientist then explained that the only way to shut down the superlaser was to destroy its power sources, citing that the AT-ST he hijacked could get him further into the facility. He pressed forward, overtaking a fleeing [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] squad and facing 10 more AT-STs and two stormtrooper-manned [[E-Web heavy repeating blaster/Legends|E-Webs]].<ref name="Rebel Strike" /> He was also strafed by TIE fighters.<ref name="RS III guide" />

{{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike}}
While navigating the docking area of the superlaser facility, Wedge made a brief detour, picking up some [[advanced concussion missile]]s.<ref name="Rebel Strike" />
{{Endgame}}

Antilles navigated the AT-ST through the superlaser facility, eventually reaching the elevator into the factory guarded by three AT-STs and stealthy [[missile droid]]s. The enemies opened fire on his walker, but he survived the travel down to the next level.<ref name="Rebel Strike" />

The basement level was full of transportation rails. Avoiding explosive containers coming at him from the rails, one of which also destroyed an enemy AT-ST that was on patrol shortly after he arrived on the floor, and engaging more AT-STs, Wedge approached a large open area. While traversing to the next lift, he also exploited the explosive containers to destroy any enemy AT-STs in his path. There, he ditched his AT-ST in the factory in favor of an [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|All Terrain Armored Transport]] with heavier firepower. With the AT-AT, Wedge was able to plow through the walkers and missile droids in his path, and he opened fire on the three turbines which were powering the superlaser, causing the facility to detonate. Making it back to his Y-Wing, Antilles and Rogue Squadron were able to escape before a chain reaction of several massive explosions pulverized the base.<ref name="Rebel Strike" />

==Aftermath==
The plot to destroy the Alliance fleet had ended in failure, and with the loss of a whole superlaser facility, the Rebels dealt a massive blow to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]].<ref name="Rebel Strike" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Retaliate against a surprise attack from a secret Imperial weapon. Identify targets of opportunity and destroy them.|Mission briefing for "Guns of Dubrillion"|<ref name="Rebel Strike" />}}
This article was created based on a mission in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike]]''. As the bonus mission ''Flight from Bespin'' branches off from the Guns of Dubrillion, it is implied to be concurrent with the [[Occupation of Bespin]] and the Duel on Cloud City.

The level was inspired by a rough sketch. In the commentary, the developers admitted that the result of the geometry was one of the best levels in the game.

The [[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|strategy guide]] suggested that the player destroy some of the AT-STs by blowing up the explosive tanks when they are directly near an AT-ST in the factory area.
